<system-reminder>[Truncated: PARTIAL view — showing lines 1-558 of 1213 total (46115 tokens, cap 25000). Call Read with offset=559 limit=558 for the next page, or Grep to find a specific section. Do NOT answer from this page alone if the answer may be further in the file.]</system-reminder>

1	\chapter{Abelian-variety rigidity: morphisms from a genus-\(0\) curve into an abelian variety are constant}
2	\label{chap:AbelianVarietyRigidity}
3	\label{chap:avr_for_rr}
4	% archon:covers AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/ChartIso.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityLemma.lean
5	% Local macro: diagrammatic (Lean `≫`) composition. Defined locally because this
6	% chapter is the writer's only editable file; flagged in the writer report for promotion
7	% to macros/common.tex if desired. \providecommand is a no-op if it is later added globally.
8	\providecommand{\fatsemi}{\mathbin{\,;\,}}
9	% STRATEGY NOTE (iter-157). This chapter is the dedicated home of the project's COMMITTED
10	% genus-0 route (route (c)): the characteristic-free abelian-variety rigidity stack. It is
11	% 1:1 with the new upstream Lean file AlgebraicJacobian/AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean, which
12	% imports AlgebraicJacobian.Genus and is imported by AlgebraicJacobian.Jacobian (breaking the
13	% RigidityKbar -> Rigidity -> Jacobian import cycle so that genusZeroWitness can consume the
14	% keystone directly). The headline prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV is the char-free
15	% replacement (no [CharZero]) for the over-k-bar declaration rigidity_over_kbar of
16	% \texttt{chap:RigidityKbar} (which remains in tree as the fallback route (a) artifact and
17	% still carries [CharZero]). The df=0 / H^0(P^1,Omega)=0 / Serre-duality framing is the
18	% fallback (a), NOT the route developed here.
19	The genus-\(0\) arm of the Jacobian-witness construction has a \emph{trivial} witness object
20	(\(J = \Spec k\)); its only remaining mathematical content is a rigidity \emph{statement}:
21	every pointed morphism from a smooth proper geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\)
22	into an abelian variety is the constant morphism at the identity. We prove this
23	\textbf{characteristic-free} --- the protected goal is over an arbitrary field \(k\), so the
24	cheaper differential argument (via \(H^0(\mathbb P^1, \Omega) = H^0(\mathbb P^1, \mathcal
25	O(-2)) = 0\), hence \(df = 0\), hence \(f\) constant) is unavailable: it breaks on a
26	characteristic-\(p\) Frobenius-descent wall, where a nonconstant \(f\) can have \(df = 0\). The
27	rigidity route below sidesteps that wall.
28	The chain has two parts. The \emph{Rigidity Lemma} (\cref{thm:rigidity_lemma}, Mumford,
29	Form~I; equivalently Milne's Rigidity Theorem~1.1) is an elementary properness/closed-map
30	argument --- it uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, is the entry point
31	for formalisation, and is now proven axiom-clean. From the Rigidity Lemma, Milne's \S I.3
32	derives the genus-\(0\) base case \emph{without} the theorem of the cube --- and, in this
33	project's reading, \emph{without} Milne's Theorem~3.2 (rational-map extension), Lemma~3.3,
34	Auslander--Buchsbaum, or differentials (the ``4th route''; see
35	\cref{rmk:base_case_fourth_route}). The decisive observation is that \(\mathbb P^1\) is
36	\emph{already complete} and \(f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) is \emph{already a total morphism}, so
37	there is no rational defect map to extend over a complete surface and Theorem~3.2 never enters.
38	Instead the base case is closed by the \textbf{\(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut} (the project's
39	primary route, an Archon-original realisation of Milne Proposition~3.9): one uses the
40	\emph{multiplicative scaling} action \(\sigma_\times \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to
41	\mathbb P^1\), \((x, \lambda) \mapsto \lambda x\) (the M\"obius scaling fixing both \(0\) and
42	\(\infty\)), which is a \emph{genuine total} scheme morphism on all of \(\mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb
43	G_m\) (\texttt{def:gaTranslationP1}). Normalising \(f(0) = \eta_A\) and composing \(h := \sigma_\times
44	\fatsemi f\), one applies additivity of \(\mathrm{Hom}(-, A)\) over a product
45	(\texttt{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}, Milne Corollary~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires
46	only the \emph{first} factor complete --- with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) proper, \(W = \mathbb G_m\), and
47	base points \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\), \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\). Because \(0\) is a \emph{fixed point} of
48	scaling, the \(W\)-axis restriction \(\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0)\) is
49	\emph{constant} at \(\eta_A\), so the decomposition collapses to \(h = p \fatsemi f\), i.e.\
50	\(f(\lambda x) = f(x)\). Specialising \(x = 1\) gives that \(f|_{\mathbb G_m}\) is constant, and since
51	\(\mathbb G_m\) is dense in \(\mathbb P^1\) with \(A\) separated, \(f\) is constant
52	(\texttt{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}, via the separated-target/dominant-source handle
53	\texttt{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen}). This route uses \emph{only} the proven Corollary~1.5, a
54	scaling fixed point, and density --- it never forms the additive defect map, never invokes
55	\(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\), and so sidesteps the ``image of a group homomorphism is a
56	closed subgroup'' input that has no Mathlib support. The classical \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive
57	argument (\texttt{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial} via the \(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility
58	\texttt{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}, the heart of Milne Proposition~3.9) is retained only as the
59	Milne-faithful alternative and is \emph{off} the genus-\(0\) critical path. The corollary that a
60	pointed regular map of abelian
61	varieties is a homomorphism (\texttt{lem:av_regular_map_is_hom}, Milne Corollary~1.2) is recorded
62	for completeness and for Route~A but is \emph{not} needed on the genus-\(0\) path; Milne's
63	Theorem~3.2 (\cref{thm:rational_map_to_av_extends}) is likewise demoted to a Route-A-only item
64	(the Albanese universal property), no longer on the genus-\(0\) critical path
65	(\cref{rmk:thm32_codim1_mathlib_gap}). Combining these
66	gives that every morphism \(\mathbb P^1 \to A\) is constant
67	(\texttt{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant}); composing with the classification of genus-\(0\)
68	curves (\texttt{thm:genus_zero_curve_iso_p1}, \(C \cong \mathbb P^1\) over \(\bar k\)) yields the
69	headline \texttt{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV}. The theorem of the cube (Milne \S I.5) is
70	\emph{not} used anywhere on this path (see \cref{rmk:cube_not_needed}); the chain invokes
71	neither Serre duality nor the FGA \(\Pic\) representability engine, so the genus-\(0\) arm is
72	fully decoupled from Route~A.
73	\medskip
74	Throughout, \(\bar k\) is an algebraically closed field of arbitrary characteristic. An
75	\emph{abelian variety} over \(\bar k\) is a complete (proper) irreducible group variety; in the
76	project's Lean encoding \(A\) is an object of \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) carrying
77	\(\mathtt{GrpObj}\), \(\mathtt{IsProper}\), \(\mathtt{Smooth}\), and \(\mathtt{GeometricallyIrreducible}\).
78	\section{The Rigidity Lemma}
79	\begin{lemma}
80	\leanok
81	[Rigidity Lemma (Mumford, Form I)]
82	  \label{thm:rigidity_lemma}
83	  \uses{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open, lem:rigidity_core, lem:rigidity_snd_lift}
84	  
85	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_lemma}
86	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
87	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let \(X\) be a complete variety, \(Y\) and \(Z\)
88	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
89	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a
90	  % morphism \(g \colon Y \to Z\) such that if \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
91	  % projection, \(f = g \circ p_2\)."
92	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43.}
93	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety, and let \(Y\), \(Z\) be any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
94	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism such that for some \(y_0 \in Y\) the image
95	  \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0 \in Z\). Then there is a morphism
96	  \(g \colon Y \to Z\) with \(f = g \circ p_2\), where \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
97	  projection; that is, \(f\) depends only on the \(Y\)-coordinate, and \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\) for any
98	  fixed \(x_0 \in X\).
99	\end{lemma}
100	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Choose any point \(x_0 \in X\), and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by
101	% \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety, to show that \(f = g \circ p_2\), it is
102	% sufficient to show that these morphisms coincide on some open subset of \(X \times Y\). Let
103	% \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), \(F = Z - U\), and \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\);
104	% then \(G\) is closed in \(Y\) since \(X\) is complete and hence \(p_2\) is a closed map. Further
105	% \(y_0 \notin G\) since \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\). Therefore \(Y - G = V\) is a non-empty
106	% open subset of \(Y\). For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
107	% \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
108	% any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
109	% assertion."
110	\begin{proof}
111	\leanok
112	% NOTE (iter-159 review): the iter-157 unsoundness REMAINS REPAIRED. The collapse hypothesis
113	% (_hf : f(X x {y0}) = {z0}) is threaded through all three helpers (rigidity_lemma ->
114	% rigidity_core -> rigidity_eqOn_dense_open) and GENUINELY CONSUMED in the body of
115	% rigidity_eqOn_dense_open (proves y0 \notin G, the load-bearing non-emptiness of V := Y - G).
116	% The counterexample f = fst is excluded (under f = fst, _hf forces X to collapse to a point).
117	% rigidity_eqOn_dense_open is sorry-free in its OWN body (hfib, the pullback-fibre fact over the
118	% k-bar-point y0, is closed). rigidity_snd_lift and snd_left_isClosedMap are axiom-clean (no sorryAx).
119	% NOTE (iter-160): the \uses edges run FORWARD along the true Lean dependency order
120	% (rigidity_lemma -> rigidity_eqOn_dense_open -> rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine), so the
121	% not-proven status of the residual sorry propagates up and de-launders BOTH thm:rigidity_lemma and
122	% lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open. No backward edge from the leaf lemmas back up the chain.
123	% NOTE (iter-161): bridge 2 (rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine) was DECOMPOSED into two named
124	% top-level sub-lemmas along the route-B (cohomology-free) split:
125	%   - Step 2, morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints: "two morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a
126	%     reduced Jacobson source into a separated target are equal" -- PROVEN axiom-clean (the
127	%     dense-closed-points coproduct probe + ext_of_isDominant; the connective Mathlib lacks).
128	%   - Step 1, rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine: Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy
129	%     -- the genuinely-deep residual, still `sorry`. THIS is now the chain's lone deep residual
130	%     (bridge 2's body is real assembly: Step 2 proven, the JacobsonSpace U instance now derivable).
131	% SIGNATURE CHANGE (iter-161, plan-authorized): Step 2's globalisation needs the closed points of
132	% the saturated open U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson, which over Spec k-bar follows from (X tensor Y)
133	% being LOCALLY OF FINITE TYPE. So [LocallyOfFiniteType (X tensor Y).hom] is now carried on FIVE
134	% chain lemmas (rigidity_lemma, rigidity_core, rigidity_eqOn_dense_open,
135	% rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine, rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine), in
136	% addition to [IsAlgClosed kbar]. Free downstream: curves and abelian varieties are of finite type
137	% over k-bar. The earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim is hereby retired.
138	  Fix any point \(x_0 \in X\) and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\) (the
139	  composite of \(y \mapsto (x_0, y)\) with \(f\)). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety (in
140	  particular reduced and irreducible, so separated and with no embedded structure), to prove
141	  the equality of morphisms \(f = g \circ p_2\) it suffices to prove they agree on a non-empty
142	  open subset of \(X \times Y\).
143	  Let \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), put \(F = Z - U\) (a closed set),
144	  and set \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\). Because \(X\) is complete, the projection
145	  \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a \emph{closed} map (this is the defining property of
146	  completeness/properness); hence \(G\), the image of the closed set \(f^{-1}(F)\), is closed in
147	  \(Y\). The hypothesis \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U\) gives
148	  \(f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing\), so \(y_0 \notin G\). Therefore
149	  \(V := Y - G\) is a non-empty open subset of \(Y\).
150	  For each \(y \in V\) we have \(X \times \{y\} \cap f^{-1}(F) = \varnothing\), i.e.
151	  \(f(X \times \{y\}) \subseteq U\). Thus the complete (proper) variety \(X \times \{y\}\) is
152	  mapped by \(f\) into the \emph{affine} variety \(U\). A proper connected variety mapping to an
153	  affine variety has image a single point (a global regular function on a complete connected
154	  variety is constant). Hence \(f(X \times \{y\})\) is one point, necessarily
155	  \(f(x_0, y) = g(y)\). So for all \(x \in X\) and \(y \in V\),
156	  \[ f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g(y) = (g \circ p_2)(x, y). \]
157	  The two morphisms \(f\) and \(g \circ p_2\) thus agree on the non-empty open
158	  \(X \times V\), which is dense in the irreducible \(X \times Y\); as \(Z\) is separated they
159	  agree everywhere. \qedhere
160	\end{proof}
161	\begin{remark}
162	  \label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_cube_free}
163	  The proof of \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma} uses \emph{only} (i) that completeness of \(X\) makes
164	  \(p_2\) a closed map, and (ii) that a proper connected variety has no nonconstant map to an
165	  affine variety. It uses neither the theorem of the cube nor any cohomology, and is valid in
166	  arbitrary characteristic. This is the designated formalisation entry point: it is the
167	  lowest-prerequisite link of the chain. (Mumford gives this as the geometric heart of the
168	  ``second proof'' of commutativity of an abelian variety; Milne packages the same statement
169	  as the Rigidity Theorem~1.1, requiring \(V\) complete and \(V \times W\) geometrically
170	  irreducible.)
171	\end{remark}
172	\begin{remark}
173	  \label{rmk:rigidity_lemma_decomposition}
174	  The Lean formalisation of \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma} splits into three helper steps, mirroring
175	  the proof above: \texttt{rigidity\_snd\_lift} (the cartesian-monoidal algebra identifying the
176	  collapsed map \((x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\) followed by \(f\) with \(g \circ p_2\));
177	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} (the scheme-level gluing: two maps out of the geometrically irreducible
178	  reduced \(X \times Y\) into the separated \(Z\) that agree on a non-empty open subset agree
179	  everywhere, by separatedness); and \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open}
180	  (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}, the genuine geometry: the construction of the non-empty
181	  open \(X \times V\) and the slice-constancy of \(f\) on it). The collapse hypothesis
182	  \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\) must be threaded through \emph{all three} helpers, since it is
183	  what makes \(V\) non-empty; a decomposition that drops it from the lower helpers is unsound
184	  (see \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}).
185	  \emph{Status (iter-161).} Bridge~1 (the closed-map step) is \textbf{built} as the axiom-clean
186	  helper \texttt{snd\_left\_isClosedMap}. The fibre-over-\(y_0\) fact is \textbf{closed} (via the
187	  coarse \texttt{PullbackCarrier} layer), so \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open} is
188	  \texttt{sorry}-free in its own body. Bridge~2 (slice-constancy on the saturated open \(U\),
189	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}) has now been \textbf{decomposed} along its
190	  cohomology-free route-(c) split into two named sub-lemmas: the globalisation
191	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} (Step~2, ``agree at every closed point of a reduced
192	  Jacobson source \(\Rightarrow\) equal'') is \textbf{proven axiom-clean}, and the per-closed-slice
193	  constancy \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine} (Step~1, Mumford's slice
194	  argument) is the chain's \emph{single genuinely-deep residual} \texttt{sorry}. The body of
195	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} is therefore real assembly (Step~2 over Step~1).
196	  % NOTE: (iter-162 review) STALE as of iter-162 — Step~1 is now PROVEN and the WHOLE
197	  % Rigidity-Lemma chain (thm:rigidity_lemma … lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine,
198	  % via the new helper lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral) is sorry-free + axiom-clean
199	  % ({propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}, re-verified). The "single genuinely-deep residual
200	  % sorry" wording here and in the proof blocks of lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open / …saturated… /
201	  % …eqAt_closedPoint… should be refreshed by the plan/blueprint-writer to "chain closed iter-162".
202	  This route requires the base field to be algebraically closed \emph{and} the source to be of finite
203	  type: Step~2 needs the closed points of \(U\) to be dense (the Jacobson-space property), which over
204	  the algebraically closed \(\bar k\) holds because \(X \times Y\) is locally of finite type.
205	  Accordingly the formalization carries \textbf{both} \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) and
206	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\) across the chain lemmas
207	  (\texttt{rigidity\_lemma}, \texttt{rigidity\_core}, \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_dense\_open},
208	  \texttt{rigidity\_eqOn\_saturated\_open\_to\_affine},
209	  \texttt{rigidity\_eqAt\_closedPoint\_of\_proper\_into\_affine}); the earlier claim that
210	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) is the \emph{only} added instance is retired. Both cost nothing
211	  downstream: curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over the algebraically closed \(\bar k\),
212	  and the consumers already assume it. The relative Stein-factorisation / proper-pushforward
213	  \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) framing is a genuine Mathlib cohomology gap and is \emph{deliberately
214	  avoided}.
215	\end{remark}
216	\begin{lemma}\leanok
217	[Cartesian-monoidal collapse identity]
218	  \label{lem:rigidity_snd_lift}
219	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_snd_lift}
220	  Let \(X\), \(Y\) be objects over \(\bar k\) and fix a \(\bar k\)-point \(x_0\) of \(X\).
221	  Post-composing the second projection \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) with the slice section
222	  \(y \mapsto (x_0, y)\) equals the ``collapse the \(X\)-coordinate onto \(x_0\)'' endomorphism
223	  \((x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\) of \(X \times Y\). This is pure cartesian-monoidal algebra (no
224	  geometry): the product universal property distributes \(p_2\), the \(Y\)-component simplifies by
225	  the identity law, and the \(X\)-component collapses by uniqueness of maps to the terminal object.
226	\end{lemma}
227	\begin{lemma}\leanok
228	[Bridge~1: completeness makes the projection a closed map]
229	  \label{lem:snd_left_isClosedMap}
230	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.snd_left_isClosedMap}
231	  \uses{lem:projectiveLineBar_isProper}
232	  Let \(X\) be complete (proper) over \(\bar k\) and \(Y\) any object over \(\bar k\). Then the
233	  underlying topological map of the second projection \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a
234	  \emph{closed} map. This is Mumford's ``completeness of \(X\) makes \(p_2\) a closed map'': the
235	  projection is the base change of \(X \to \Spec \bar k\) along \(Y \to \Spec \bar k\), properness
236	  of \(X\) gives universal closedness, and universal closedness is stable under base change, so the
237	  base-changed projection is universally closed and hence a closed map. Characteristic-free; no
238	  theorem of the cube, no cohomology.
239	\end{lemma}
240	\begin{lemma}\leanok
241	[Scheme-level gluing core of the Rigidity Lemma]
242	  \label{lem:rigidity_core}
243	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_core}
244	  \uses{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
245	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed, \(X\) proper, \(X \times Y\) geometrically irreducible,
246	  reduced and locally of finite type over \(\bar k\), and \(Z\) separated. Let
247	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) collapse the slice \(X \times \{y_0\}\) to a single point \(z_0\).
248	  Then \(f\) equals its composite with the collapse endomorphism \((x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\), i.e.\
249	  \(f = \mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\). The proof takes the non-empty open \(U\) on which \(f\) and
250	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}); since \(X \times Y\)
251	  is geometrically irreducible over the one-point base \(\Spec \bar k\), it is irreducible, so the
252	  non-empty open \(U\) is dense and its inclusion is dominant; the dominant-source/separated-target
253	  rigidity handle then promotes agreement on \(U\) to equality of the two morphisms everywhere.
254	\end{lemma}
255	\begin{lemma}
256	\leanok
257	[Dense-open agreement: the geometric heart of the Rigidity Lemma]
258	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
259	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_dense_open}
260	  \uses{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine, lem:snd_left_isClosedMap}
261	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
262	  % SOURCE QUOTE: "RIGIDITY LEMMA. (Form I.) Let \(X\) be a complete variety, \(Y\) and \(Z\)
263	  % any varieties, and \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) a morphism such that for some
264	  % \(y_0 \in Y\), \(f(X \times \{y_0\})\) is a single point \(z_0\) of \(Z\). Then there is a
265	  % morphism \(g \colon Y \to Z\) such that if \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is the
266	  % projection, \(f = g \circ p_2\)."
267	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
268	  dense-open construction is the body of that proof).}
269	  Let \(X\) be a complete variety and \(Y\), \(Z\) any varieties over \(\bar k\). Let
270	  \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a point \(x_0 \in X\), and suppose given
271	  \(y_0 \in Y\), \(z_0 \in Z\) with the \emph{collapse hypothesis}
272	  \[ f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \qquad
273	     \text{(in Lean: } \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathbf 1_X)\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi y_0) \fatsemi f
274	     = \mathtt{toUnit}\,X \fatsemi z_0\text{).} \]
275	  Then there is a non-empty open subset \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) on which \(f\) agrees, as a
276	  scheme morphism, with the collapsed map \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\), where
277	  \(\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,(\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,
278	  (p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y)\) is the ``\((x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\)'' endomorphism. Concretely
279	  \(U = X \times V\) with \(V := Y - G\), \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(Z - U_0))\) for an affine open
280	  neighbourhood \(U_0\) of \(z_0\), and on \(U\) one has \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y)\).
281	  \medskip
282	  \noindent\textbf{The collapse hypothesis is load-bearing.} The non-emptiness of \(V\) is exactly
283	  the assertion \(y_0 \notin G\), and this holds \emph{because} \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}
284	  \subseteq U_0\) keeps the slice over \(y_0\) inside the affine \(U_0\), away from \(F = Z - U_0\). A
285	  version of this lemma \emph{without} the collapse hypothesis is false: for \(X = Y = Z\) and
286	  \(f = p_1\) the first projection, \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f \colon (x, y) \mapsto x_0\)
287	  agree on no non-empty open (the agreement locus is contained in \(\{x = x_0\}\), which has empty
288	  interior in the irreducible \(X \times Y\)). Therefore the formal target \emph{must} carry the
289	  collapse hypothesis as an explicit antecedent; dropping it (as an earlier decomposition
290	  silently did) makes the deferred goal unsatisfiable.
291	  \medskip
292	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization note: algebraically closed base, and locally-of-finite-type
293	  source.} The prose already takes \(\bar k\) algebraically closed, but the formal statement must
294	  \emph{encode} two properties. First, the cohomology-free proof of the slice-constancy bridge
295	  (below) needs each per-closed-point residue field \(\kappa(y)\) to be \(\bar k\) itself, so that a
296	  proper integral slice has global sections \(\Gamma = \bar k\) and maps to a single \(\bar k\)-point of
297	  the affine \(U_0\); this requires \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\). Second, the globalisation of the
298	  per-slice agreement (Step~2 of the bridge, \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}) needs the
299	  closed points of the saturated open \(U\) to be \emph{dense} --- i.e.\ \(U\) to be a Jacobson space.
300	  Over the algebraically closed \(\bar k\) this holds precisely because \(X \times Y\) is \emph{locally of
301	  finite type} over \(\bar k\) (\texttt{LocallyOfFiniteType.jacobsonSpace} transports the Jacobson
302	  property of \(\Spec \bar k\), and \texttt{closure\_closedPoints} then gives density); this requires
303	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\). Accordingly the formalization carries
304	  \emph{both} \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) and
305	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\) on this lemma (in addition to
306	  \([\mathtt{Field}\ \bar k]\)), and propagates the same two instances to \cref{thm:rigidity_lemma},
307	  its core helper \texttt{rigidity\_core}, and the bridge sub-lemmas
308	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} and
309	  \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}. Both cost nothing downstream: the
310	  consumers \texttt{prop:morphism_P1_to_AV_constant} and \texttt{prop:rigidity_genus0_curve_to_AV} (Lean
311	  \texttt{rigidity\_genus0\_curve\_to\_grpScheme}) \emph{already} assume
312	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) --- the base-field variable is literally named \(\mathtt{kbar}\) ---
313	  and curves and abelian varieties are of finite type over \(\bar k\), so the
314	  locally-of-finite-type instance is automatic there.
315	\end{lemma}
316	% SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "PROOF. Choose any point \(x_0 \in X\), and define \(g \colon Y \to Z\) by
317	% \(g(y) = f(x_0, y)\). Since \(X \times Y\) is a variety, to show that \(f = g \circ p_2\), it is
318	% sufficient to show that these morphisms coincide on some open subset of \(X \times Y\). Let
319	% \(U\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), \(F = Z - U\), and \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\);
320	% then \(G\) is closed in \(Y\) since \(X\) is complete and hence \(p_2\) is a closed map. Further
321	% \(y_0 \notin G\) since \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\}\). Therefore \(Y - G = V\) is a non-empty
322	% open subset of \(Y\). For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
323	% \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
324	% any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
325	% assertion."
326	\begin{proof}
327	  \leanok
328	  Let \(U_0\) be an affine open neighbourhood of \(z_0\) in \(Z\), put \(F = Z - U_0\), and set
329	  \(G = p_2(f^{-1}(F))\). Because \(X\) is complete, the projection \(p_2 \colon X \times Y \to Y\) is a
330	  \emph{closed} map, so \(G\) (the image of the closed set \(f^{-1}(F)\)) is closed in \(Y\). The
331	  collapse hypothesis \(f(X \times \{y_0\}) = \{z_0\} \subseteq U_0\) gives
332	  \(f^{-1}(F) \cap (X \times \{y_0\}) = \varnothing\), hence \(y_0 \notin G\); this is the only place
333	  the collapse hypothesis is used, and it is essential, for \(V := Y - G\) is non-empty precisely
334	  because \(y_0 \in V\). For each \(y \in V\) the proper variety \(X \times \{y\}\) is mapped by \(f\)
335	  into the affine \(U_0\), hence to a single point, necessarily \(f(x_0, y)\). So for all \(x \in X\),
336	  \(y \in V\), we have \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x, y)\), i.e.\ \(f\) and
337	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on the non-empty open \(U := X \times V\). (In the Lean
338	  encoding the two slice-constancy bridges are the closed-map step from properness and the
339	  ``proper-into-affine has single-point image'' step; see the docstring of
340	  \texttt{rigidity\_core}.)
341	  \medskip
342	  \noindent\textbf{Formalization notes (the concrete char-free Mathlib route).} The textbook
343	  proof above is realised in Lean by three pieces. They cite Mathlib lemma names as the chosen
344	  route; none uses cohomology, and the relative Stein-factorisation framing is deliberately
345	  \emph{avoided} (see below).
346	  \emph{Bridge~1 (the closed-map step) --- BUILT.} ``\(X\) complete \(\Rightarrow p_2\) closed'' is
347	  the axiom-clean helper \texttt{snd\_left\_isClosedMap} (iter-158). It is obtained from
348	  \texttt{IsProper.toUniversallyClosed} on \(X\), base-changed across the canonical pullback square
349	  to make \(p_2\) universally closed, using that the monoidal projection \emph{is} the chosen
350	  pullback projection: \texttt{Over.snd\_left} rewrites \((\mathtt{snd}\,X\,Y).\mathtt{left}\) to
351	  \(\mathtt{Limits.pullback.snd}\ X.\mathtt{hom}\ Y.\mathtt{hom}\) exactly. This step is already
352	  proven.
353	  \emph{The fibre fact (non-emptiness of \(V\)) --- RESOLVED route, char-free.} That the slice
354	  \(X \times \{y_0\}\) over the \(\bar k\)-rational point \(y_0\) is exactly the image of the slice
355	  section \(s \colon X \to X \times Y\) is formalised \emph{without} the residue-field /
356	  \texttt{Triplet} / tensor-product machinery (that fine \texttt{carrierEquiv} layer is the wrong
357	  granularity and is to be avoided). One pastes the identity square for the section against the
358	  canonical pullback square (\texttt{IsPullback.of\_right},
359	  \texttt{IsPullback.of\_horiz\_isIso}; the section identities \(s \fatsemi p_1 = \mathbf 1\) and
360	  \(s \fatsemi p_2 = X.\mathtt{hom} \fatsemi y_0\) come from \texttt{lift\_fst}/\texttt{lift\_snd}
361	  with \texttt{Over.w} on the unit) and reads off the fibre with the \emph{coarse} topological
362	  layer of \texttt{PullbackCarrier}, namely
363	  \texttt{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Pullback.image\_preimage\_eq\_of\_isPullback}. This needs no
364	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}]\).
365	  \emph{Bridge~2 (slice-constancy / the agreement equation) --- RESOLVED route, cohomology-FREE.}
366	  ``Proper connected slice into affine \(\Rightarrow\) single point, glued to a morphism equality on
367	  \(U\)'' is realised by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route, \emph{not} by any
368	  relative Stein-factorisation or proper-pushforward \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) statement: the
369	  latter is a genuine Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no \(H^0\) base change), and route~(c)
370	  exists precisely to avoid coherent cohomology, so it must \emph{not} be attempted. The concrete
371	  stack is: for each closed point \(y \in V\) one has \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\) (alg-closed, finite type);
372	  the slice \(X_y\) is proper integral, so its global sections form a field
373	  (\texttt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}) module-finite over \(\bar k\)
374	  (\texttt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}), and algebraic closedness kills the finite
375	  extension to give \(\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k\). A morphism into the affine \(U_0\) is pinned by its ring
376	  map on global sections (\texttt{ext\_of\_isAffine}), so \(f\) and
377	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on each closed slice. Closed points are dense in the
378	  locally-of-finite-type \(U\) (\texttt{closure\_closedPoints} / the Jacobson-space property of
379	  locally-of-finite-type \(\bar k\)-schemes; in the formalization this density is supplied by the new
380	  hypothesis \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\)), so the per-slice
381	  agreement globalises to all of \(U\) via the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity
382	  \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant\_of\_isSeparated'} (the same handle \texttt{rigidity\_core} already
383	  uses). The packaged ``morphisms agreeing on a dense set of closed points are equal''
384	  hom-extensionality --- the one connective Mathlib does not supply directly (it gives only the
385	  single-dominant-morphism \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant}) --- has now been built as the axiom-clean
386	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} (Step~2): it assembles the closed points into one
387	  dominant coproduct probe and applies \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant}. The whole argument is extracted
388	  into the dedicated obligation \cref{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine} below, whose body
389	  wires that proven Step~2 over the per-slice Step~1
390	  (\cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}); Step~1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice
391	  constancy) is now the chain's \emph{single} genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
392	\end{proof}
393	\begin{lemma}
394	\leanok
395	[Bridge~2: slice-constancy on a saturated open mapping into an affine]
396	  \label{lem:rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
397	
398	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.rigidity_eqOn_saturated_open_to_affine}
399	  \uses{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine, lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
400	  % NOTE (iter-161): the iter-160 SIGNATURE GAP is RESOLVED. The Lean proof wires Step 2
401	  % (morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints, PROVEN axiom-clean) over Step 1
402	  % (rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine, the residual sorry). Step 2 needs the closed
403	  % points of U to be DENSE, i.e. U Jacobson; over k̄ this follows from (X⊗Y) being locally of finite
404	  % type, so [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom] is now a stated hypothesis of this lemma (and across the
405	  % chain). With it, the `JacobsonSpace U` instance is a routine discharge (LocallyOfFiniteType.
406	  % jacobsonSpace + JacobsonSpace.of_isOpenEmbedding), no longer an as-typed gap. The only `sorry`
407	  % reachable from this lemma is now Step 1 (Mumford's per-closed-slice constancy). The hypothesis
408	  % set is recorded in the statement and proof prose below (both [IsAlgClosed k̄] AND
409	  % [LocallyOfFiniteType (X⊗Y).hom]); the earlier "[IsAlgClosed] is the only added instance" claim
410	  % is retired.
411	  % SOURCE: [Mumford, Abelian Varieties], Rigidity Lemma (Form I.), Ch.~II \S4, book p.~43 (read from references/mumford-abelian-varieties.pdf, PDF page 54)
412	  % SOURCE QUOTE PROOF: "For each \(y \in V\), the complete variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by
413	  % \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single point of \(U\). But this means that for
414	  % any \(x \in X\), \(y \in V\), \(f(x, y) = f(x_0, y) = g \circ p_2(x, y)\), and this proves our
415	  % assertion." [Mumford, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma, p.~43.]
416	  \textit{Source: Mumford, Abelian Varieties, Ch.~II \S4, Rigidity Lemma (Form~I), p.~43 (the
417	  ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete slice maps into the affine, hence to a single point'' step).}
418	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and let \(X\), \(Y\), \(Z\) be objects of
419	  \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\) with \(X\) proper, \(X \times Y\) geometrically irreducible, reduced,
420	  and \emph{locally of finite type} over \(\bar k\) (the formalization carries
421	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\), which makes the saturated open \(U\)
422	  below a Jacobson space, so its closed points are dense --- the hypothesis Step~2 of the proof
423	  consumes), and \(Z\) separated. Let \(f \colon X \times Y \to Z\) be a morphism, fix a \(\bar k\)-point
424	  \(x_0 \colon \mathbf 1 \to X\), and write \(\mathtt{retract} := \mathtt{lift}\,
425	  (\mathtt{toUnit}\,(X \times Y) \fatsemi x_0)\,(p_2)\) for the ``\((x, y) \mapsto (x_0, y)\)''
426	  endomorphism. Suppose:
427	  \begin{itemize}
428	    \item \(U \subseteq X \times Y\) is a \(p_2\)-\emph{saturated} open, i.e.\ \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\)
429	      for a set \(V_{\mathrm{set}} \subseteq Y\) (so \(U\) contains the whole fibre \(X_y\) over each of
430	      its points);
431	    \item \(U_0 \subseteq Z\) is an \emph{affine} open;
432	    \item \(f(U) \subseteq U_0\), i.e.\ \(f\) maps \(U\) into the affine \(U_0\).
433	  \end{itemize}
434	  Then \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree on \(U\) as scheme morphisms:
435	  \(U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)\).
436	\end{lemma}
437	\begin{proof}
438	  \leanok
439	  This is the formalization realization of Mumford's step ``for each \(y \in V\), the complete
440	  variety \(X \times \{y\}\) gets mapped by \(f\) into the affine variety \(U\), and hence to a single
441	  point of \(U\)''. We realise it by a \textbf{global-sections + per-closed-point} route that is
442	  \emph{cohomology-free}; in particular the relative Stein-factorisation / proper-pushforward
443	  \(f_*\mathcal O = \mathcal O\) framing is a confirmed Mathlib gap (no Stein factorisation, no
444	  \(H^0\) base change) and is \emph{deliberately avoided}. The argument splits into two named
445	  sub-lemmas, one residual and one proven.
446	  \medskip
447	  \noindent\textbf{Step 1 (per-closed-slice constancy) --- \cref{lem:rigidity_eqAt_closedPoint_of_proper_into_affine}, the residual.}
448	  Fix a closed point \(x\) of \(U\), lying over a closed point \(y = p_2(x) \in V_{\mathrm{set}}\).
449	  Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and \(Y\) is of finite type over \(\bar k\), the residue field
450	  is \(\kappa(y) = \bar k\). Because \(U = p_2^{-1}(V_{\mathrm{set}})\) is saturated, \(U\) contains the
451	  \emph{entire} fibre \(X_y \cong X\) over \(y\); hence \(f\) maps the proper integral slice \(X_y\) into the
452	  affine \(U_0\). By \(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\) the global sections \(\Gamma(X_y)\) form a
453	  field, module-finite over \(\bar k\) by \(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\), and
454	  algebraic closedness collapses the finite extension to give \(\Gamma(X_y) = \bar k\). A morphism into
455	  the affine \(U_0\) is pinned by its ring map on global sections (\(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\)), so the
456	  restriction of \(f\) to \(X_y\) factors through a single \(\bar k\)-point of \(U_0\) --- necessarily the
457	  point \(f(x_0, y) = (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)(x)\). Thus \(f\) and \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\)
458	  agree (after the residue-field probe) at every closed point of \(U\). This per-slice statement is the
459	  chain's single genuinely-deep residual \texttt{sorry}.
460	  \medskip
461	  \noindent\textbf{Step 2 (globalisation over dense closed points) --- \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, proven.}
462	  The closed points are dense in the locally-of-finite-type \(\bar k\)-scheme \(U\)
463	  (\(\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}\) / the Jacobson-space property, supplied here by the new hypothesis
464	  \([\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\ (X \otimes Y).\mathrm{hom}]\)). To turn ``\(f\) and
465	  \(\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f\) agree at each closed point'' into a single morphism equality on \(U\),
466	  the proven globalisation \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints} assembles the closed points
467	  into one dominant probe --- the coproduct
468	  \(\coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,U} \Spec \kappa(x) \to U\), whose range is dense --- and feeds
469	  it to the reduced-source / separated-target rigidity handle \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) (the same
470	  family \(\mathtt{rigidity\_core}\) uses). This yields the morphism equality
471	  \(U.\iota \fatsemi f = U.\iota \fatsemi (\mathtt{retract} \fatsemi f)\) on all of \(U\). The
472	  ``agrees on a dense set of closed points \(\Rightarrow\) hom-equality'' connective is the one piece
473	  Mathlib does not package directly (it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
474	  \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\)); that bespoke build is now done in
475	  \cref{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}, and it is \emph{not} cohomology. \qedhere
476	\end{proof}
477	\begin{lemma}\leanok
478	[Step~2: morphisms agreeing at every closed point of a Jacobson source are equal]
479	  \label{lem:morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
480	  
481	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_eq_of_eqAt_closedPoints}
482	  % Project-bespoke assembly: NO external source. Mathlib supplies only the single-dominant-morphism
483	  % ext_of_isDominant; the "dense closed points => hom-extensionality" connective is built here.
484	  Let \(W\), \(Z\) be schemes with \(W\) \emph{reduced}, \(W\) a \emph{Jacobson space}, and \(Z\)
485	  \emph{separated}. Let \(g_1, g_2 \colon W \to Z\) be morphisms. If, for every \emph{closed} point
486	  \(x \in W\), the two morphisms agree after restriction along the residue-field point
487	  \(\Spec \kappa(x) \to W\) (in Lean, \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 =
488	  W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2\)), then \(g_1 = g_2\).
489	\end{lemma}
490	\begin{proof}
491	\leanok
492	  This is the ``dense closed points \(\Rightarrow\) hom-extensionality'' connective that Mathlib does
493	  not package directly: it supplies only the single-dominant-morphism extensionality
494	  \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) (a dominant morphism into a separated target is an epimorphism for
495	  the purpose of right-cancellation). We assemble the per-closed-point hypotheses into one such
496	  dominant probe.
497	  Form the coproduct \(P := \coprod_{x \in \mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} \Spec \kappa(x)\) and the morphism
498	  \(\mathrm{probe} \colon P \to W\) defined by \(\mathtt{Sigma.desc}\) of the residue-field points
499	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\). Its topological range contains every closed point of \(W\):
500	  the inclusion \(\iota_x \colon \Spec \kappa(x) \to P\) composes with \(\mathrm{probe}\) to
501	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\) (the defining property of \(\mathtt{Sigma.desc}\)), and the
502	  range of \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x\) is the singleton \(\{x\}\)
503	  (\(\mathtt{Scheme.range\_fromSpecResidueField}\)). Because \(W\) is a Jacobson space, the closed points
504	  are dense (\(\mathtt{closure\_closedPoints}\), i.e.\ \(\overline{\mathrm{closedPoints}\,W} = W\)), so
505	  the range of \(\mathrm{probe}\) is dense and \(\mathrm{probe}\) is \emph{dominant}.
506	  Componentwise, \(g_1\) and \(g_2\) agree after \(\mathrm{probe}\): precomposing with each coproduct
507	  inclusion \(\iota_x\) reduces (by \(\mathtt{Sigma.\iota\_desc}\)) to
508	  \(W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_1 = W.\mathtt{fromSpecResidueField}\,x \fatsemi g_2\),
509	  which is the hypothesis at the closed point \(x\). By the universal property of the coproduct
510	  (\(\mathtt{Sigma.hom\_ext}\)) this gives \(\mathrm{probe} \fatsemi g_1 = \mathrm{probe} \fatsemi g_2\).
511	  Since \(\mathrm{probe}\) is dominant and \(Z\) is separated, \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isDominant}\) cancels it
512	  on the left to yield \(g_1 = g_2\).
513	\end{proof}
514	\begin{lemma}
515	\leanok
516	[Global-sections constancy: two \(\bar k\)-points of a proper integral source into an affine agree]
517	  \label{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
518	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}
519	  % Project-bespoke assembly of Mathlib lemmas: NO external source. Realises, cohomology-free, the
520	  % classical "a global regular function on a proper integral variety over an algebraically closed
521	  % field is constant" --- precisely the affine-image step of Mumford's Rigidity Lemma proof quoted
522	  % above (the "complete slice into an affine maps to a single point" sentence, Ch.~II \S4, p.~43).
523	  Let \(\bar k\) be algebraically closed and let \(W\) be an integral scheme equipped with a structure
524	  morphism \(w \colon W \to \Spec \bar k\) that is universally closed (proper) and locally of finite
525	  type. Let \(g \colon W \to V\) be a morphism into an \emph{affine} scheme \(V\). Then any two
526	  \(\bar k\)-points of \(W\) --- i.e.\ any two sections \(a, b \colon \Spec \bar k \to W\) of \(w\) (so
527	  \(a \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1\) and \(b \fatsemi w = \mathbf 1\)) --- satisfy \(a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g\).
528	  \medskip
529	  \noindent\textbf{Every hypothesis is load-bearing} (confirmed by the iter-161 audit).
530	  \([\mathtt{IsAlgClosed}\ \bar k]\) collapses the finite extension \(\Gamma(W)/\bar k\); integrality of
531	  \(W\) makes \(\Gamma(W)\) a domain (without it a two-point disjoint union is a counterexample);
532	  \(\mathtt{UniversallyClosed}\) is what upgrades \(\Gamma(W)\) to a \emph{field} (without it
533	  \(\mathbb A^1\), with \(\Gamma = \bar k[t]\) and two distinct \(\bar k\)-points, is a counterexample);
534	  \(\mathtt{LocallyOfFiniteType}\) gives finiteness of \(\Gamma(W)\) over \(\bar k\); and
535	  \([\mathtt{IsAffine}\ V]\) is what lets \(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\) pin the morphism by its ring map
536	  on global sections.
537	\end{lemma}
538	\begin{proof}
539	\leanok
540	  Because \(W\) is integral and \(w\) is universally closed, the global sections
541	  \(\Gamma(W, \mathcal O_W)\) form a field (\(\mathtt{isField\_of\_universallyClosed}\)), and the structure
542	  ring map \(w^\sharp \colon \bar k \to \Gamma(W)\) (the map \(\mathtt{wk.appTop}\) after the canonical
543	  identification \(\Gamma(\Spec \bar k) \cong \bar k\)) is integral / module-finite
544	  (\(\mathtt{finite\_appTop\_of\_universallyClosed}\)). Since \(\bar k\) is algebraically closed and
545	  \(\Gamma(W)\) is a domain finite over it, that map is bijective
546	  (\(\mathtt{IsAlgClosed.ringHom\_bijective\_of\_isIntegral}\)); thus \(\Gamma(W) = \bar k\) and
547	  \(w^\sharp\) is an isomorphism on global sections --- in particular an epimorphism. Both sections
548	  \(a\), \(b\) left-invert \(w^\sharp\) on global sections (they are sections of \(w\)), so they induce the
549	  \emph{same} ring map \(a^\sharp = b^\sharp\) on \(\Gamma(W)\) (cancel the epimorphism \(w^\sharp\)). A
550	  morphism into the \emph{affine} \(V\) is pinned by its global-sections ring map
551	  (\(\mathtt{ext\_of\_isAffine}\)), whence \(a \fatsemi g = b \fatsemi g\). This realises, without
552	  cohomology, the classical ``proper connected variety into an affine is a single point'' step
553	  quoted from Mumford above.
554	\end{proof}
555	\begin{lemma}
556	\leanok
557	[Integrality descends to a retract of an integral scheme]
558	  \label{lem:isIntegral_of_retract_of_integral}

text:
Base directory for this skill: /AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/blueprint-conventions

Blueprints are a LaTeX subset (the `leanblueprint` dialect) parsed into the DAG.
Write timeless mathematics — a standalone document, not a project journal.

## Environments

Each declaration is an amsthm-style environment; the recognized ones are
`theorem`, `lemma`, `proposition`, `corollary`, `definition`, `remark`,
`example`, `notation`, `convention`, and `proof`. A statement environment plus
its following `proof` environment form one node's content.

## Per-node macros

- `\label{id}` — a stable id (the DAG node key).
- `\lean{Name}` — the Lean declaration this node formalizes. Keep it 1-to-1: one
  blueprint node ↔ one Lean declaration, signatures matching.
- `\uses{a,b}` — dependencies on other nodes' labels; these drive the DAG edges.
  Add an edge instead of re-explaining a dependency in prose. Put `\uses` in the
  statement for definitional deps and in the `proof` for deps used only in the
  proof.
- `\source{slug:page-0001}` — reference anchors for UI popups and audit trails.
  Use page-level ids from `references/<slug>/tex/page-0001.tex` or an equivalent
  manifest entry. Multiple anchors may be comma-separated.
- `\leanok` — the Lean is written and checked. Honest only when its Lean is
  actually checked *and* its dependencies are too. On a `proof`, it means the
  proof is formalized; on a statement, the signature is formalized.
- `\mathlibok` — the declaration is already available in mathlib (no
  formalization needed; treat as a leaf).

## House style

- **Pure mathematics only.** No Lean tactics, typeclass/implementation notes, or
  semi-Lean pseudocode. No project history ("since iter N", "our failed route")
  and no conversational filler. If a Lean helper exists with no math counterpart,
  write its LaTeX statement so the 1-to-1 correspondence holds.
- **Complete proofs, not sketches.** A node's `proof` is a real, rigorous
  mathematical proof a reader could check — not a hand-wave or a TODO. The way to
  keep it short is to *split*, not to abbreviate: if a proof is long or a node
  grows past a short paragraph, break out the hard step as its own `\uses`-linked
  lemma (down to sentence-sized lemmas if needed) so each node stays small and
  its proof stays complete.
- **One declaration's worth per node.** Don't restate Lean source as prose.
- **Cite by source link, not quote comments.** Add `\source{...}` to the
  relevant node. Keep the retrieved/transcribed page text in `references/` (for
  example `references/<slug>/tex/page-0042.tex`) and let the UI expose it; do not
  paste verbatim source quotes into `% QUOTE` comments inside the blueprint.

950	  \textit{Source: Milne, Abelian Varieties, Proposition~3.9.}

952	  \emph{completeness} proof as the abstract argument, then record Milne's elementary \(\mathbb
953	  G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility, which applies whenever \(\varphi\) extends to a morphism on
954	  \(\mathbb P^1\) (as it does in the genus-\(0\) application).

956	  (image of the connected \(\mathbb G_a\) under a homomorphism). As the image of a homomorphism of
957	  algebraic groups it is a \emph{closed} subgroup of \(A\), hence \emph{complete} (closed in the
958	  complete \(A\)); as a homomorphic image of the \emph{affine} \(\mathbb G_a\) it is moreover
959	  \emph{affine}. A connected variety that is simultaneously complete and affine over \(\bar k\) is a
960	  single point: its ring of global regular functions is a field finite over \(\bar k\), hence equal
961	  to \(\bar k\) (the \(\Gamma = \bar k\) collapse already packaged for proper integral sources in
962	  \cref{lem:eq_comp_of_isAffine_of_properIntegral}). Therefore \(\varphi(\mathbb G_a)\) is one point;
963	  being a homomorphism, \(\varphi\) sends \(0\) there, so the point is \(0_A\) and
964	  \(\varphi = \mathtt{toUnit}\,\mathbb G_a \fatsemi \eta_A\).

966	  \noindent\textbf{Milne's elementary \(\mathbb G_a/\mathbb G_m\) incompatibility (when \(\varphi\)
967	  extends to \(\mathbb P^1\)).} Suppose \(\varphi = f|_{\mathbb G_a}\) for a morphism
968	  \(f \colon \mathbb P^1 \to A\) (the genus-\(0\) situation). Set \(g := f - f(1)\), so \(g(1) = 0_A\), and
969	  form \(h_\times := \sigma_\times \fatsemi g \colon \mathbb P^1 \times \mathbb G_m \to A\) with the
970	  scaling companion \(\sigma_\times\) of \texttt{def:gaTranslationP1}. Applying the additive
971	  decomposition \texttt{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) (complete),
972	  \(W = \mathbb G_m\), base points \(1 \in \mathbb P^1\) and \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\) (so
973	  \(h_\times(1, 1) = g(1) = 0_A\)), the axis restrictions are \(x \mapsto g(x)\) and

1105	  using the multiplicative scaling action \(\sigma_\times\) of \texttt{def:gaTranslationP1} (a genuine
1106	  total morphism --- no rational-map extension needed). Apply the additive decomposition
1107	  \texttt{lem:hom_additivity_over_product} (Milne Cor~1.5) --- whose Lean signature requires only the
1108	  \emph{first} factor complete --- with \(V = \mathbb P^1\) (proper), \(W = \mathbb G_m\), and base
1109	  points \(0 \in \mathbb P^1\), \(1 \in \mathbb G_m\); here \(h(0, 1) = f(1 \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A\), as
1110	  required. The decomposition writes \(h = (p \fatsemi f_0) + (q \fatsemi g)\) with \(f_0\) the
1111	  \(V\)-axis restriction \(x \mapsto h(x, 1) = f(1 \cdot x) = f(x)\) (so \(f_0 = f\)) and \(g\) the
1112	  \(W\)-axis restriction \(\lambda \mapsto h(0, \lambda) = f(\lambda \cdot 0) = f(0) = 0_A\).
1113	  \textbf{The \(W\)-axis collapses (the load-bearing scaling fixed point).} Because \(0\) is a
1114	  \emph{fixed point} of scaling, \(\sigma_\times(0, \lambda) = 0\) for all \(\lambda\)
1115	  (\texttt{lem:gmScaling_fixes_zero}), so the \(W\)-axis
1116	  restriction \(g\) is the \emph{constant} morphism at \(0_A\) --- the identity of the hom-group
1117	  \(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_m, A)\). The corresponding summand \(q \fatsemi g\) is therefore the group
1118	  identity, and the decomposition collapses to
1119	  \[ h = p \fatsemi f, \qquad\text{i.e.}\qquad
1120	     \sigma_\times \fatsemi f = \mathrm{pr}_1 \fatsemi f, \qquad
1121	     \text{``}f(\lambda x) = f(x)\text{''} \quad (x \in \mathbb P^1,\ \lambda \in \mathbb G_m). \]
1122	  \textbf{Constancy via density.} Specialise \(x = 1\) (precompose with \(\lambda \mapsto (1,
1123	  \lambda)\)): \(f(\lambda) = f(1)\) for all \(\lambda \in \mathbb G_m\), i.e.\ \(f|_{\mathbb G_m}\) is
1124	  the constant morphism at \(f(1)\). Since \(\mathbb G_m\) is dense in the irreducible \(\mathbb P^1\)
1125	  and \(A\) is separated, the two morphisms \(f\) and \(\mathrm{const}_{f(1)}\), agreeing on the
1126	  non-empty open \(\mathbb G_m\), agree everywhere by the dominant-source/separated-target rigidity
1127	  handle \texttt{thm:GrpObj_eq_of_eqOnOpen} (the same \texttt{ext\_of\_isDominant} family
1128	  \texttt{rigidity\_core} uses). Hence \(f\) is constant, with value \(f(1)\); in the normalised
1129	  picture \(f(0) = 0_A\), so the constant is \(0_A\) (the original \(f(0)\) before translation).
1130	  \medskip
1131	  \noindent\emph{Multi-factor remark.} For the general unirational statement of Proposition~3.10,
1132	  one writes \(\beta(x_1, \ldots, x_d) = \sum_i \beta_i(x_i)\) via the additive decomposition
1133	  (\texttt{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) and applies this base case to each \(\beta_i \colon
1134	  \mathbb P^1 \to A\); we need only the \(d = 1\) case here. The Milne-faithful \(\mathbb
1135	  G_a\)-additive route (\texttt{lem:hom_from_Ga_trivial}, via \(\mathrm{Hom}(\mathbb G_a, A) = 0\),
1136	  \texttt{lem:hom_Ga_to_av_trivial}) reaches the same conclusion and is retained as an alternative,
1137	  but is \emph{not} on this critical path.
1138	  % NOTE (iter-166 review): The Lean factors the proof into the outer translation reduction
1139	  % (`AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const`, public) plus a private basepoint helper
1140	  % (`AlgebraicGeometry.morphism_P1_to_grpScheme_const_aux`) carrying the body of the
1141	  % \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling shortcut. The chapter presents the proof at a single granularity; the
1142	  % Lean split is structural-only and preserves the chapter's logical content. The helper carries
1143	  % five honest scaffold sorries (three \(\mathbb P^1 \otimes \mathbb G_m\) product-instance
1144	  % obligations [GeometricallyIrreducible / LocallyOfFiniteType / IsReduced], one
1145	  % \(\mathtt{IsReduced}\ (\mathbb P^1)_{\text{left}}\) obligation, and one
1146	  % \(\mathtt{IsDominant}\ \iota_{\mathbb G_m}.\text{left}\) obligation — the latter strictly
1147	  % downstream of \(\sigma_\times\) landing a body); these are Mathlib bridge work and a Lane-2
1148	  % dependency, not laundered hypotheses. Both `lean-vs-blueprint-checker-avr-iter166` and
1149	  % `lean-auditor-iter166` confirmed: no iter-157-style laundering, basepoint hypothesis \(hf_0\)
1150	  % is genuinely consumed (L975 of the Lean: `rw [← Category.assoc, hcollapse]; exact hf0`),
1151	  % `lean_verify` axioms $\{\mathtt{propext}, \mathtt{sorryAx}, \mathtt{Classical.choice},
1152	  % \mathtt{Quot.sound}\}$ propagate cleanly. Lifts to axiom-clean once the 5 helper sorries
1153	  % discharge.
1154	  % NOTE (iter-167 review): the 5 helper sorries above have been reduced to ONE. The four
1155	  % product / \(\mathbb P^1\) instance obligations are now discharged via Lane A's named exports
1156	  % (`projGm_geomIrred`, `projGm_locallyOfFiniteType`, `projGm_isReduced`,

756	\section{The genus-\(0\) base objects and the \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling action (the primary route)}
757	\begin{definition}
758	[Concrete genus-\(0\) base objects: \(\mathbb P^1\), \(\mathbb G_a\), \(\mathbb G_m\) over \(\bar k\)]
759	  \label{def:genus0_base_objects}
760	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveLineBar}
761	  % Archon-original Lean encoding of standard objects; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
762	  % I.2--3 for the projective line; the group laws on \(\mathbb A^1\) are elementary). No external
763	  % % SOURCE QUOTE is needed for the Lean encoding itself.
764	  Work over \(\Spec \bar k\) with \(\bar k\) algebraically closed. The primary \(\mathbb G_m\)-scaling
765	  route (and its \(\mathbb G_a\)-additive alternative) needs only three concrete objects of
766	  \(\mathrm{Over}\,(\Spec \bar k)\):
767	  \begin{itemize}
768	    \item \(\mathbb P^1 = \mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\), the projective line --- a smooth proper
769	      geometrically irreducible curve of genus \(0\) (Mathlib: projective space, or \(\mathrm{Proj}\)
770	      of the graded polynomial ring; \lean name \texttt{ProjectiveLineBar}). It carries
771	      the distinguished \(\bar k\)-points \(0\), \(1\), \(\infty\) and the two standard affine charts
772	      \(\mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\) (coordinate \(x\)) and
773	      \(\mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0\}\) (coordinate \(u = 1/x\)).
774	    \item \(\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1_{\bar k}\), the affine line as the \emph{additive} group object
775	      (Mathlib: \texttt{AffineSpace}\,\(\mathbb A(1; \Spec \bar k)\) with the \(\mathtt{GrpObj}\)
776	      additive structure \((x, y) \mapsto x + y\), identity \(0\); \lean name \texttt{Ga}).
777	      As a scheme \(\mathbb G_a = \mathbb A^1 = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{\infty\}\), and the open
778	      immersion \(\mathbb G_a \hookrightarrow \mathbb P^1\) has dense (cofinite) image.
779	    \item \(\mathbb G_m = \mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\} = \mathbb P^1 \setminus \{0, \infty\}\), the
780	      \emph{multiplicative} group object (group law \((x, y) \mapsto xy\), identity \(1\); \lean name
781	      \texttt{Gm}). Its open immersion into \(\mathbb P^1\) also has dense image.
782	  \end{itemize}
783	  All three are of finite type over \(\bar k\); \(\mathbb P^1\) is proper, while \(\mathbb G_a\) and
784	  \(\mathbb G_m\) are affine (hence not complete). These are the only ``genus-\(0\)'' geometric inputs
785	  the base case needs.
786	\end{definition}
787	% Per-decl Lean coverage hooks for the components of \cref{def:genus0_base_objects}. The bundled
788	% definition above pins \texttt{ProjectiveLineBar} in its own \lean{...} block; the sibling
789	% definition blocks below pin the remaining Lean targets one-by-one so the blueprint-doctor can
790	% statically link each declaration.
791	% NOTE (iter-172 encoding clarification): the Lean encoding of \(\mathbb G_m\) realises
792	% \(\mathbb A^1 \setminus \{0\}\) as the AFFINE scheme \(\Spec(\mathrm{Localization.Away}\,t)\)
793	% rather than as the basic open subscheme \(D(t) \subset \mathbb A^1\). The two are
794	% canonically isomorphic; the affine-Spec choice is preferred in this project because
795	% downstream consumers (the scaling action \(\sigma_\times\) of
796	% \texttt{def:gaTranslationP1}, the additivity decomposition input
797	% \(W = \mathbb G_m\) of \texttt{lem:hom_additivity_over_product}) need an affine scheme,
798	% not just an open subscheme of one.
799	\begin{lemma}
800	[\(\mathbb P^1_{\bar k}\) is proper over \(\Spec \bar k\)]
801	  \label{lem:projectiveLineBar_isProper}
802	  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.projectiveLineBar_isProper}
803	  \uses{def:genus0_base_objects}
804	  % Archon-original instance; the mathematics is textbook (Hartshorne
805	  % II.4 + the standard properness of Proj of a finitely generated graded
806	  % algebra over a Noetherian base; here the base is a field, hence trivially
807	  % Noetherian). No external SOURCE QUOTE needed.
808	  The structure morphism \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k).\mathrm{hom}
809	  \colon \mathbb P^1_{\bar k} \to \Spec \bar k\) is proper.
810	  \medskip
811	  \noindent\textbf{Proof sketch.} Unfold \((\mathtt{ProjectiveLineBar}\,\bar k)
812	  .\mathrm{hom}\) as the composition
813	  \(\Proj.\mathrm{toSpecZero}\,(\mathrm{projectiveLineBarGrading}\,\bar k)
814	  \fatsemi \Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{algebraMap}\,\bar k\,
815	  \uparrow{\mathcal A_0})\). The first factor is proper by Mathlib's
816	  \(\mathtt{Proj.instIsProperToSpecZero}\): the polynomial algebra
817	  \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) is finite type over its degree-\(0\) piece
818	  \(\mathcal A_0\), and the \(\mathtt{IsScalarTower}\) +
819	  \(\mathtt{Algebra.FiniteType}\) chain (via
820	  \(\mathtt{Algebra.FiniteType.of\_restrictScalars\_finiteType}\)) supplies the
821	  finite-type witness. The second factor \(\Spec.\mathrm{map}\,(\mathrm{algebraMap}
822	  \,\bar k\,\uparrow{\mathcal A_0})\) is an isomorphism: the algebra map
823	  \(\bar k \to \uparrow{\mathcal A_0}\) is bijective because \(\mathcal A_0\)
824	  is the constants subalgebra of \(\bar k[X_0, X_1]\) (injectivity via
825	  \(\mathtt{MvPolynomial.C\_injective}\); surjectivity via
826	  \(\mathtt{MvPolynomial.homogeneousComponent\_of\_mem}\) +
827	  \(\mathtt{homogeneousComponent\_zero}\)). Composition of proper with iso is
828	  proper. LOC: \(\sim 40\) (already landed axiom-clean).
829	\end{lemma}
830	\subsection*{Chart cover and chart-ring iso (formalisation infrastructure)}

465	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`): for any field extension `K/k̄`, the base change
466	`ℙ¹_K` is covered by the two pulled-back chart opens `p ⁻¹ᵁ D₊(X i)`, each irreducible
467	(`≅ 𝔸¹_K = Spec (K[u])`), and the two chart opens intersect (both contain any preimage

471	/-- **Topological input**: a space covered by two irreducible opens with nonempty
472	intersection is irreducible. -/
473	private lemma irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens {α : Type*} [TopologicalSpace α]
474	    {U V : Set α} (_hUo : IsOpen U) (hVo : IsOpen V)
475	    (hU : IsIrreducible U) (hV : IsIrreducible V)
476	    (hUV : (U ∩ V).Nonempty) (hcov : ∀ x : α, x ∈ U ∨ x ∈ V) :
477	    IrreducibleSpace α := by
478	  obtain ⟨w, hwU, hwV⟩ := hUV
479	  refine { toNonempty := ⟨w⟩, isPreirreducible_univ := ?_ }
480	  intro u v hu hv hune hvne
481	  obtain ⟨a, -, hau⟩ := hune
482	  obtain ⟨b, -, hbv⟩ := hvne
483	  rw [Set.univ_inter]
484	  -- Any nonempty open meeting `U` also meets `V` (through `U`'s irreducibility and
485	  -- the common point `w`); hence both `u` and `v` meet `V`, and `V`'s irreducibility
486	  -- closes the goal.
487	  have key : ∀ {u' : Set α}, IsOpen u' → ∀ {c : α}, c ∈ u' → c ∈ U →
488	      (U ∩ (u' ∩ V)).Nonempty := fun {u'} hu' {c} hcu' hcU =>
489	    hU.2 u' V hu' hVo ⟨c, hcU, hcu'⟩ ⟨w, hwU, hwV⟩
490	  have hu_meets_V : (V ∩ u).Nonempty := by
491	    rcases hcov a with haU | haV
492	    · obtain ⟨c, -, hcu, hcV⟩ := key hu hau haU
493	      exact ⟨c, hcV, hcu⟩
494	    · exact ⟨a, haV, hau⟩
495	  have hv_meets_V : (V ∩ v).Nonempty := by
496	    rcases hcov b with hbU | hbV
497	    · obtain ⟨c, -, hcv, hcV⟩ := key hv hbv hbU
498	      exact ⟨c, hcV, hcv⟩
499	    · exact ⟨b, hbV, hbv⟩
500	  obtain ⟨c, -, hcu, hcv⟩ := hV.2 u v hu hv hu_meets_V hv_meets_V
501	  exact ⟨c, hcu, hcv⟩

503	/-- **Ring-level input**: the chart ring `Away 𝒜 (X i) ≅ k̄[u]` stays a domain after base
504	change to any field extension `K/k̄` (via `k̄[u] ⊗[k̄] K ≅ K[u]`). -/
505	private lemma chartAway_tensor_isDomain (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2)
506	    (K : Type u) [Field K] [Algebra kbar K] :

509	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) K) := by

511	  haveI hMv : IsDomain (TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K) := by
512	    haveI : IsDomain (MvPolynomial Unit K) := inferInstance
513	    have e2 : TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K ≃+*
514	        TensorProduct kbar K (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :=
515	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K).toRingEquiv
516	    have e1 : TensorProduct kbar K (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit K :=
517	      (MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv kbar K).toRingEquiv
518	    exact Function.Injective.isDomain (e1.toRingHom.comp e2.toRingHom)
519	      (e1.injective.comp e2.injective)
520	  -- Transport along `congr` of the chart-ring iso (upgraded to a `kbar`-algEquiv).
521	  let eUnit : HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
522	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) ≃ₐ[kbar] MvPolynomial Unit kbar :=

525	  have e : TensorProduct kbar

527	        (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)) K ≃+*
528	      TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K :=
529	    (Algebra.TensorProduct.congr eUnit AlgEquiv.refl).toRingEquiv
530	  exact Function.Injective.isDomain e.toRingHom e.injective

562	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)

568	(a domain), which does not contain the irrelevant ideal (`X 0 ≠ 0`). Typed as a point of

571	    ↥(ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar) :=
572	  show ProjectiveSpectrum (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) from
573	    ⟨⊥, Ideal.isPrime_bot, fun hle => by
574	      have h0 := hle (HomogeneousIdeal.mem_irrelevant_of_mem _ Nat.zero_lt_one

577	      exact MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero (R := kbar) _ h0⟩

593	/-- **`ℙ¹_{k̄}` is geometrically irreducible over `Spec k̄`.** Formerly a BareScheme
594	scaffold sorry; relocated here (iter-201) with the real proof via the 2-chart cover:

598	instance projectiveLineBar_geomIrred (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
599	    GeometricallyIrreducible (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom := by
600	  rw [geometricallyIrreducible_iff]
601	  intro K _ y Z fst snd h
602	  suffices hmain : IrreducibleSpace ↥(pullback (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom y) by
603	    exact h.isoPullback.hom.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff.mpr hmain
604	  -- Notation: `p` is the base-change projection `ℙ¹_K ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}`; `c i` the chart
605	  -- open immersion `Spec (Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⟶ ℙ¹_{k̄}`; `V i` the pulled-back chart opens.
606	  let p : pullback (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom y ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
607	    pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom y

609	    (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)

628	        TopologicalSpace.Opens ↥(pullback (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom y)) :
629	        Set ↥(pullback (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom y))

502	instance gm_grpObj (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : GrpObj (Gm kbar) :=
503	  GrpObj.ofRepresentableBy (Gm kbar) (gmHomFunctor kbar) (gmHomFunctor_representableBy kbar)

505	open scoped TensorProduct in
506	/-- **Ring-level input for `gm_geometricallyReduced`:** for every field extension `K/k̄` the
507	base change `k̄[t,t⁻¹] ⊗[k̄] K` is reduced. Route: `GmRing ⊗[k̄] K` is (via
508	`Algebra.TensorProduct.cancelBaseChange` + `comm`) the localization of
509	`P ⊗[k̄] K ≅ MvPolynomial Unit K` — a domain, hence reduced — at the image of the powers of
510	`t` (`IsLocalization.tensor`), and localization preserves reducedness
511	(`isReduced_localizationPreserves`). -/
512	private lemma gmRing_tensor_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (K : Type u) [Field K]
513	    [Algebra kbar K] : _root_.IsReduced (GmRing kbar ⊗[kbar] K) := by
514	  -- `P ⊗[k̄] K ≅ MvPolynomial Unit K` is a domain, hence reduced.
515	  have hPK : _root_.IsReduced (MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] K) := by
516	    have e : (MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] K) ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit K :=
517	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) K).toRingEquiv.trans
518	        (MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv kbar K).toRingEquiv
519	    exact isReduced_of_injective e.toRingHom e.injective
520	  -- Its localization at (the image of) the powers of `t` is reduced.
521	  have hLoc : _root_.IsReduced
522	      ((MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] K) ⊗[MvPolynomial Unit kbar] GmRing kbar) := by
523	    exact isReduced_localizationPreserves
524	      (Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid (MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] K)
525	        (Submonoid.powers (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)))
526	      ((MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] K) ⊗[MvPolynomial Unit kbar] GmRing kbar) hPK
527	  -- Transfer along `GmRing ⊗[k̄] K ≅ GmRing ⊗[P] (P ⊗[k̄] K) ≅ (P ⊗[k̄] K) ⊗[P] GmRing`.
528	  have e2 : (GmRing kbar ⊗[kbar] K) ≃+*
529	      ((MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] K) ⊗[MvPolynomial Unit kbar] GmRing kbar) :=
530	    ((Algebra.TensorProduct.cancelBaseChange kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar)
531	        (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) K).symm.trans
532	      (Algebra.TensorProduct.comm (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
533	        (MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] K))).toRingEquiv
534	  exact isReduced_of_injective e2 e2.injective
535	
536	open scoped TensorProduct in
537	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
538	/-- **`𝔾_m` is geometrically reduced over `Spec k̄`.** For each field extension `K/k̄` the
539	pullback is `Spec(GmRing ⊗[k̄] K) = Spec(K[t,t⁻¹])` (via `pullbackSpecIso`), reduced by
540	`gmRing_tensor_isReduced` (localization of the domain `K[t]`). -/
541	instance gm_geometricallyReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
542	    GeometricallyReduced (Gm kbar).hom := by
543	  rw [geometricallyReduced_iff]
544	  intro K _ y Z fst snd h
545	  obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y
546	  algebraize [φ.hom]
547	  have h' : IsPullback fst snd
548	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))))
549	      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar K))) := h
550	  haveI : IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (GmRing kbar ⊗[kbar] K))) := by
551	    rw [affine_isReduced_iff]
552	    exact gmRing_tensor_isReduced kbar K
553	  exact isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion
554	    (h'.isoPullback ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso kbar (GmRing kbar) K).hom
555	
556	/-- **`𝔾_m` is smooth over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from `smooth_of_grpObj` once
557	`GrpObj`, `LocallyOfFinitePresentation`, and `IsReduced` are installed. -/
558	instance gm_smooth (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar] :
559	    Smooth (Gm kbar).hom :=
